highlights.title: UAE's MGX raises one of the biggest AI funds ever as it closes at $49 billion
highlights.description: The company is a backer of major AI companies including OpenAI and Anthropic, alongside Elon Musk's xAI, prior to its merger with SpaceX.

highlights.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
highlights.description:
After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access Wednesday to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline.
Anthropic wrote:
We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
highlights.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

highlights.title: Anthropic Says US Lifted Restrictions on Fable 5
highlights.description: The US government removed foreign access restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 artificial intelligence model, clearing it for wider distribution. Anthropic said it had received notice that Commerce was lifting the curbs on access to Mythos 5 and Fable 5 AI models. Some of the restrictions on Mythos had been eased on June 26. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Google and Actor Idris Elba to Fund African Creator Access to AI
highlights.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google and UK actor Idris Elba will fund access to artificial intelligence tools for African creators, with the goal of helping them produce high-quality content faster and more cheaply.

highlights.title: Claude Fable 5 cleared to return as US lifts Anthropic’s export control restriction
highlights.description: Anthropic has confirmed it will begin restoring access to Claude Fable 5 this Wednesday, July 1, nearly three weeks after US export controls forced the company to take the model offline. Here are the details.

highlights.title: Cleared by the US, derailed by the UK: Getty’s Shutterstock merger falls apart
highlights.description:
Getty says it’s “not required” to accept approval conditions that would force Shutterstock to offload its editorial business. | Image: The Verge	

Getty is planning to axe its $3.7 billion merger agreement with Shutterstock after a UK regulator imposed restrictions that would prevent part of Shutterstock's business from being included in the deal. The move comes despite the US Department of Justice granting the deal "unconditional antitrust clearance" in February.
In an SEC filing published on Tuesday in the US, Getty said it is "not required to accept" approval conditions outlined by the UK Competition and Markets Authority in May that require Shutterstock to sell its global editorial business, including the Backgrid and Splash paparazzi agencies.
Those conditions have proved unappe …
Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: ByteDance Picks Brazil for Its Largest Data Center Outside China
highlights.description: The TikTok creator’s $39 billion data center is the latest payoff from Lula’s courtship of Chinese investment.

highlights.title: TikTok Eyes About 300 More Job Cuts at European Hub in Dublin
highlights.description: TikTok is considering cutting about 300 jobs at its European hub in Dublin, following a similarly sized round of layoffs last year.

highlights.title: Uber-Backed Bike Firm Lime, Investors Raise $174 Million in IPO
highlights.description: Neutron Holdings Inc., better known as Lime, and some of its shareholders raised $174 million in a US initial public offering that priced at the midpoint of the marketed range.

ai.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
ai.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

ai.title: Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival
ai.description: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.

ai.title: Gemini Spark comes to Google's Gemini app for macOS
ai.description: The Gemini macOS app now has access to Google's Spark agentic AI assistant.

ai.title: US government allows Anthropic to redeploy its Mythos and Fable AI models
ai.description: Anthropic will start its users' access to Mythos and Fable tomorrow, July 1.

ai.title: Agilent CEO on Business Strategy, Innovation
ai.description: Padraig McDonnell, President and CEO at Agilent, discusses the company's business strategy and the innovations he sees in the life sciences sector. He speaks with Minmin Low from the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Anthropic restores AI models Fable, Mythos after the U.S. lifts export controls
ai.description: The government cleared Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 30, weeks after a cybersecurity finding triggered an export order that froze access for everyone.

ai.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
ai.description:
Would you pay $20 a month for access to AI hardware you already own? That appears to be one of Meta's next bets. This week, it quietly announced that your glasses' Conversation Focus feature will soon be limited to three hours of use per month, unless you pay for a $19.99 Meta One Premium subscription. 
In a help article, the company insists that it won't require a subscription to use your glasses, period; it's merely erecting a "rate limit" for certain AI features. Even premium subscribers will only get 15 hours of Conversation Focus per month under that "rate limit," it claims.
Problem is, Meta's rate limit is ridiculous. The Conversatio …
Read the full story at The Verge.

ai.title: Abu Dhabi’s MGX Raises $49 Billion for One of the Biggest Ever AI Funds
ai.description: MGX has raised $49 billion for one of the biggest ever funds dedicated to artificial intelligence deals, propelling the two-year-old Abu Dhabi firm into the ranks of the most consequential investors in the sector globally.

ai.title: OCBC to Raise Annual Tech Spending to More Than $771 Million
ai.description: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp.  plans to increase its annual technology spending, including on artificial intelligence, to more than S$1 billion ($771 million), said a top executive.

ai.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
ai.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

ai.title: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Show AI Impact on Labor
ai.description: Whether artificial intelligence will cause mass workforce cuts over time remains up for debate, but it is starting to leave an imprint on US employment data.

ai.title: An English Furniture Maker Faces AI Era of Bots Buying Sofas
ai.description: With origins in the English countryside, The Cotswold Company is known for upscale furniture that evokes its bucolic backstory. Now it’s bracing for the era of artificial intelligence.

ai.title: Japan Investment Corp.'s Hata on AI, Strategic Sectors
ai.description: Yuka Hata, Senior Managing Director & Head of Fund Investments at Japan Investment Corporation, says demand for capital is growing as Japan shifts its focus toward physical AI and deep technologies. She adds that Japan’s labor shortages have heightened the need for AI-driven solutions, making the sector a key investment priority for JIC. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Panasonic Targets Further AI Growth to Build on Record Valuation
ai.description: Panasonic Holdings Corp., the Japanese electronics conglomerate, is attracting renewed investor attention as a beneficiary of the artificial-intelligence boom.

ai.title: Samsung drops first official Galaxy Z Fold 8 teasers, ‘New Shape, New Joy’ [Gallery]
ai.description: With less than a month to go, Samsung is kicking off its train of Galaxy Z Fold 8 teasers with a new series of social posts that also purged nearly the company’s entire Instagram account.

ai.title: A Giant Cable Exposes Ireland’s AI Ambitions and Security Risks
ai.description: A new Amazon transatlantic fiber-optic link is symbolic of the country’s tech economy, but also of its chronic lack of defense spending.

business.title: Vimeo Parent Bending Spoons, Backers Raise $1.68 Billion in IPO
business.description: Bending Spoons SpA, which acquires struggling software businesses, and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion in a US initial public offering priced above a marketed range.

ev_autonomous.title: Rivian R2 spotted with LiDAR near HQ, looks better than most LiDARs
ev_autonomous.description: Rivian’s new R2 has been spotted near Rivian HQ sporting a LiDAR sensor, which won’t be included with the earliest vehicles but will be added in a few months. And the implementation actually looks pretty good, compared to the large “taxi bumps” present on many other vehicles.

ev_autonomous.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
ev_autonomous.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.
